summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple drone attacks on infrastructure in Kyiv region, Borispil, and Kramatorsk, causing injuries and fires. Russian forces limited fuel sales in Moscow and St. Petersburg regions, indicating logistical strain. The United States advanced a $8 billion military credit package for Ukraine, pending final Senate vote.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck a Borispil industrial site in Kyiv region, injuring a worker and prompting fire suppression efforts (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
Russian authorities imposed gasoline and diesel purchase limits of 100‑150 L per person in Moscow and 50‑100 L in St. Petersburg, citing market conditions (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548).
U.S. House passed a bill authorising $8 billion in military credits for Ukraine and new sanctions against Russia, with final vote scheduled for 7 August (Source: @UkraineNow/81657).
Two major UAV groups were reported over the Sumy front, advancing up to 700 m in multiple sectors and engaging in infantry battles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian drone attacks injured civilians in Belgorod region, including an 11‑year‑old boy in Bochkovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile strike near Kharkiv, prompting air defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67476).
A resident of Sevastopol was detained for allegedly passing information about Russian servicemen to Ukrainian intelligence (Source: @tass_agency/378963).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones attacked a Borispil industrial facility in Kyiv region, injuring a worker and igniting fires. Russian fuel distribution limits in Moscow and St. Petersburg signal domestic logistical pressures. The U.S. advanced a substantial $8 billion military credit package for Ukraine, awaiting final Senate approval.

points: Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on an industrial object in Borispil, Kyiv region, wounding a plant employee and requiring fire‑extinguishing operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
Women in the Nikolaev region reportedly used shovels to drive away military commissars, indicating civilian resistance activities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30647).
Ukrainian troops on the Sumy front advanced up to 700 m in eleven sectors, engaging in infantry battles around Bachevsk, Ivolzhansk, and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian units on the Kharkiv front pushed toward the logistics hub at Kachya Lopan, fighting in villages such as Shevchenko and Okhrymovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile impact near Kharkiv, triggering air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67476).
Ukrainian drone activity was noted over the northern‑western Chernihiv region, heading south toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63857).
Ukrainian drone groups were observed over the Sumy‑Khar‑Kursk border areas, moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/63838).
Russian authorities limited gasoline sales to 100‑150 L per person in Moscow and 50‑100 L in St. Petersburg, with stricter limits at some stations (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548).
A Sevastopol resident was detained for allegedly transmitting information about Russian servicemen to Ukrainian intelligence (Source: @tass_agency/378963).
Russian drone attacks in Belgorod region injured civilians, including an 11‑year‑old boy in Bochkovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian forces reported a 6‑year‑old girl injured by drone debris in Bryansk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian officials announced the procurement of 11 domestically produced Tu‑214 aircraft for commercial use (Source: @tass_agency/378946).
Russian officials discussed a new cyber‑crime prevention law at the PMEF (Source: @tass_agency/378968).
Russian officials claimed Ukrainian officials were misappropriating Western tax‑payer money (Source: @tass_agency/378944).
Ukrainian troops on the Sumy front advanced up to 700 m in eleven sectors, with ongoing battles in Bachevsk, Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, Lesnoye, and Taratutino (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported progress in the Kupyansk‑Uzlovoe direction, indicating a south‑eastern push (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian drone attacks in the Belgorod region injured civilians near Shebekino and Bochkovka, showing cross‑border strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drone activity was observed from the north‑west of Chernihiv moving toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63857).
Ukrainian drone groups were reported near the Sumy‑Khar‑Kursk border, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/63838).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a multi‑story building in Tambov region, causing damage to a library and an industrial plant (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone attack on a passenger train in northern Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drones were reported over the north of Zhytomyr heading toward Korosten (Source: @kpszsu/63877).
Ukrainian reactive UAVs were observed moving toward Kyiv from the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/63875).
Ukrainian drone activity noted on the north‑west of Chernihiv, heading south toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63857).
Russian fuel restrictions in Moscow and St. Petersburg suggest logistical strain on northern Russian forces (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548).
A 6‑year‑old girl injured by drone debris in Bryansk region indicates Russian northern‑western exposure to Ukrainian UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drone attacks damaged a civilian car in the Lgovsky district of Kursk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a commercial facility in Shebekino, Belgorod region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a passenger train in northern Crimea, with combat continuing overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drones attacked a residential building in Kramatorsk, causing three deaths and four injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81650).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile threat to Poltava, Odesa, Mykolaiv, Sumy, and Kharkiv regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67479).
Ukrainian drone activity was noted over the southern Kupyansk‑Uzlovoe direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Progress plant in Mikhailovsk, Rostov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drones were observed over the southern Rostov region, Millerovo district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported explosions in Odessa, Zaporozhye, and Nikolaev during the night (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
At least 30 enemy drones were shot down over Leningrad region, with 22 intercepted near the capital (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian drones struck a Borispil industrial site in Kyiv region, causing injury and fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
Ukrainian drone attacks injured civilians in Belgorod region, including an 11‑year‑old boy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Ukrainian forces reported a ballistic missile impact near Kharkiv, prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67476).
Ukrainian drone activity over the north‑west of Chernihiv moved south toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63857).
Ukrainian drones were observed over the Sumy‑Khar‑Kursk border heading south (Source: @kpszsu/63838).
Ukrainian drones attacked a passenger train in northern Crimea, with ongoing combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones struck a Borispil industrial facility in Kyiv region, injuring a worker and igniting fires, prompting emergency response (Source: @UkraineNow/81655).
Russian authorities imposed gasoline purchase limits of 100‑150 L per person in Moscow and 50‑100 L in St. Petersburg, reflecting fuel distribution challenges (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548).
The U.S. House approved a bill authorising $8 billion in military credits for Ukraine and new sanctions against Russia, with final Senate vote set for 7 August (Source: @UkraineNow/81657).
Ukrainian forces advanced up to 700 m on the Sumy front across eleven sectors, engaging in infantry battles around Bachevsk and Ivolzhansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian drone attacks in Belgorod region injured civilians, including an 11‑year‑old boy in Bochkovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
A Sevastopol resident was detained for allegedly passing information about Russian servicemen to Ukrainian intelligence (Source: @tass_agency/378963).
Ukrainian drone activity over the north‑west of Chernihiv moved south toward Kyiv region, indicating continued aerial pressure on the capital (Source: @kpszsu/63857).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations continue to target Russian‑held infrastructure in the north, east, and southern fronts, demonstrating sustained offensive capability. Russian logistical strain is evident from fuel rationing in major western cities, potentially limiting operational tempo. The U.S. financial commitment, if enacted, could bolster Ukrainian defence but may also provoke intensified Russian counter‑measures.
sections.regional_response: Western allies, notably the United States, are moving toward a substantial $8 billion credit package for Ukraine, indicating strong political support. Russia's internal measures, such as fuel limits, suggest a focus on domestic stability amid the conflict. Neighboring NATO states remain vigilant, with no direct military involvement reported in the sourced messages.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian forces are leveraging drone swarms to strike logistical nodes and civilian infrastructure within Russian‑occupied territories, achieving tactical gains on multiple fronts. Russian civilian authorities are employing economic controls that could affect military logistics, especially in the western regions. Monitoring of U.S. legislative progress is critical, as the timing of the aid package will influence Ukrainian operational planning.
